code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to produce a phrase (without repeating the subject) for each predicate
    def phrase(triple: Triple) -> str:
        p, o = triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Patterns that describe the work (or its author) without restating the subject
        patterns = {
            "author": f"was written by {o}",
            "isbnNumber": f"has ISBN {o}",
            "followedBy": f"is followed by {o}",
            "precededBy": f"was preceded by {o}",
            "language": f"is written in {o}",
            "mediaType": f"is available as {o}",
            "numberOfPages": f"contains {o} pages",
            "country": f"was published in {o}",
            "almaMater": f"the author studied at {o}",
            "doctoralAdvisor": f"the author was advised by {o}",
            "nationality": f"the author is {o}",
            "residence": f"the author lives in {o}",
            "codenCode": f"carries the CODEN {o}",
            "issnNumber": f"has ISSN {o}",
            "LCCN_number": f"has LCCN {o}",
            "abbreviation": f"is abbreviated as {o}",
            "academicDiscipline": f"belongs to the discipline of {o}",
            "publisher": f"was published by {o}",
            "firstPublicationYear": f"was first published in {o}",
            "editor": f"was edited by {o}",
            "impactFactor": f"has an impact factor of {o}",
            "oclcNumber": f"has OCLC number {o}",
            "libraryofCongressClassification": f"is classified under {o}",
            "genre": f"is a {o} work",
            "literaryGenre": f"is a {o} work",
            "eissnNumber": f"has e‑ISSN {o}",
            "frequency": f"is issued {o}",
            "headquarter": f"is headquartered in {o}",
            "leader": f"the leader is {o}",
            "birthPlace": f"the author was born in {o}",
            "affiliation": f"is affiliated with {o}",
            "city": f"is located in {o}",
            "president": f"the president is {o}",
            "state": f"is in the state of {o}",
            "spokenIn": f"is spoken in {o}",
            "influencedBy": f"was influenced by {o}",
            "family": f"belongs to the {o} family",
            "ethnicGroup": f"is associated with the {o} ethnic group",
            "largestCity": f"the largest city is {o}",
            "location": f"is located in {o}",
            "deathPlace": f"the author died in {o}",
            "notableWork": f"notable work includes {o}",
            "parentCompany": f"is owned by {o}",
            "regionServed": f"serves the region of {o}",
            "founder": f"was founded by {o}",
            "capital": f"the capital is {o}",
            "demonym": f"the demonym is {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"the leader holds the title of {o}",
            "nickname": f"is nicknamed {o}",
            "genus": f"belongs to the genus {o}",
            "releaseDate": f"was released on {o}",
            "birthDate": f"the author was born on {o}",
        }
        return patterns.get(p, f"{p} {o}")

    # Build a list of descriptive phrases for all triples
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    phrases = [phrase(t) for t in triples]

    # Combine the subject with its phrases into one fluent sentence
    if phrases:
        sentence_body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + (", and " if len(phrases) > 1 else "") + phrases[-1]
        sentence = f"{main_subject}, {sentence_body}."
    else:
        sentence = f"{main_subject}."

    # Capitalize first letter (subject may already be capitalized)
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    # Build clauses, starting with a clause that mentions the main subject
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    main_clauses = [clause(triple) for triple in triples if triple.subject == main_subject]

    # If there are other subjects, add them as additional information
    other_clauses = [clause(triple) for triple in triples if triple.subject != main_subject]

    all_clauses = main_clauses + other_clauses

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence
    if len(all_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = all_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(all_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + all_clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first letter
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
